content:
Rork complete  deployment  and verified  now FINAL SENIOR DEVELOPER EXECUTION POLICY

Stop reporting LOCAL ONLY changes as completed work.

From now on, every task must finish with executable proof.

For every task perform this sequence:

1. Modify the code.
2. Run TypeScript typecheck.
3. Run lint (if configured).
4. Run build.
5. Run automated tests.
6. Start the application.
7. Test the actual functionality.
8. Fix every error found.
9. Commit to GitHub.
10. Push to GitHub.
11. Deploy to Render/Vercel if applicable.
12. Verify production.
13. Return evidence.

Do not mark any task COMPLETE if any of the following is true:

- STATUS = LOCAL ONLY
- TESTS NOT RUN
- TYPECHECK NOT RUN
- BUILD NOT RUN
- RUNTIME NOT VERIFIED
- DEPLOYMENT NOT VERIFIED

Instead mark it:

IN_PROGRESS

or

BLOCKED_WITH_EXACT_REASON

For every task provide this evidence:

A. Files changed
B. Typecheck result
C. Build result
D. Test result
E. Runtime verification
F. GitHub commit SHA
G. GitHub branch
H. Render deployment ID (if backend changed)
I. Production URL
J. Production /health HTTP status
K. Screenshot or logs proving the feature works
L. Any remaining blockers

Do not repeat generic narratives.

Do not say VERIFIED unless you executed the verification.

If a test cannot be run, explicitly state:
- why it could not be run,
- what evidence is available,
- what remains before the task can honestly be considered complete.

Operate as a senior software engineer:
code → verify → deploy → verify production → provide evidence.

If any step is skipped, the task is not complete.
